summary: Mass decompilation pass over the item subsystem that matched all item functions of 40 bytes or less in the easy_funcs range cited by the author. The PR replaced many placeholder markers with small C wrappers, trivial callbacks, item state transitions, collision/reflect/bounce handlers, simple physics/animation functions, and small item-var accesses across 121 files. It also added or refined item attribute/item-var structs and header prototypes needed to type those functions. Review focused heavily on avoiding misleading casts, preserving correct Item_GObj arguments through tiny wrapper chains, and not letting UNK_PARAMS hide missing arguments.
what_changed: Matched the remaining item functions of size <= 40 bytes for the tool range shown in the PR body: `python tools/easy_funcs.py -v 80259869 -V 802F6507 -S 40 --names` reportedly produced no output after the change.

Converted many `/// #symbol` placeholders into small item C implementations, usually one-call wrappers or simple callbacks such as `Dropped` forwarding to `Thrown`, `Reflected` returning `it_80273030(gobj)`, `ShieldBounced` returning `itColl_BounceOffShield(gobj)`, and motion anim functions returning `it_802751D8(gobj)` or `false`.
Added many direct item state changes via `Item_80268E5C(gobj, state, ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE)` or similar constants in files such as `itbox.c`, `itevyoshiegg.c`, and `itflipper.c`.

Added small item-var and attribute struct definitions in shared item headers, including `itFireAttributes`, `itPikachutJoltAir_ItemVars`, `itDosei_ItemVars`, `itLipstickAttributes`, `itSScopeAttributes`, and `itSonans_ItemVars`.

Refined headers from generic `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` declarations to typed prototypes where small functions revealed signatures, for example changing `it_8027D820` to `void it_8027D820(Item_GObj* gobj)` in `itbombhei.h` and `it_802C1A58` to `void it_802C1A58(Item_GObj* gobj)` in `itclimbersice.h`.

Added needed includes such as `it/inlines.h`, `it/it_26B1.h`, `it/it_2725.h`, `it/item.h`, and game/header includes where newly decompiled functions used `GET_ITEM`, item callbacks, collision helpers, or external functions.

Implemented small item callback patterns around `it_8026B894(gobj, ref_gobj)` for many `EvtUnk`-style functions, with review pushing removal of unnecessary `HSD_GObj*` casts when both parameters were `Item_GObj*` or compatible.
Added a very small `itclimbersstring.c` wrapper `fn_802C28B8(Item_GObj* gobj)` that calls a static helper with `GET_ITEM(gobj)`.
smart_moves: Targeted a mechanically bounded slice of the codebase: item functions <= 40 bytes. This made a large PR reviewable because most functions were trivial wrappers, callbacks, or state transitions.
Used `easy_funcs.py` as an objective progress gate and reported the exact command whose output became empty, making the decomp milestone easy to verify.
Leveraged automated assistants for repetitive small-function decompilation but kept human oversight and review, which surfaced subtle signature and cast problems that matching alone may not catch.
Converted small matched functions into typed prototypes and shared structs instead of leaving callers behind `UNK_PARAMS`, improving future decomp readability and type propagation.
Introduced narrowly scoped item-var structs only where offsets were needed by matched functions, such as Sonans accessing `xDD4_itemVar.sonans.x60` and Dosei storing `ip->pos` into `xDE4`.

Reused recurring item idioms consistently: `Dropped` delegating to `Thrown`, event callbacks calling `it_8026B894`, reflection using `it_80273030`, shield bounce using `itColl_BounceOffShield`, and motion transitions using `Item_80268E5C`.
decomp_lessons: Do not trust successful compilation through `UNK_PARAMS`; it can hide missing arguments. Review specifically noted that `UNK_PARAMS` resolves to nothing, so calls with omitted parameters may compile even when the real callee takes a `GObj` or multiple arguments.

For tiny wrapper functions, decompile or inspect the callee to infer the wrapper signature. Reviewer r-burns used `decomp.py` on a non-decompiled callee to verify that surrounding functions also needed an `Item_GObj*` argument.

Avoid forcing matches with broad casts between GObj subtypes. Casts like `(HSD_GObj*) ref_gobj` or `(Ground_GObj*) gobj` were review red flags and often indicated that a callee signature or wrapper signature should be fixed instead.

Item callbacks commonly carry `Item_GObj* gobj` even when the body is just a forwarder. Preserve that argument through wrapper chains such as `it_80299F94` -> `it_80299FB4(gobj)` and Sonans phys functions -> `it_802CD4FC(gobj)`.

When an argument type is genuinely unknown, prefer an explicit placeholder type such as `UNK_T` over inventing an incorrect concrete pointer type. The `itseakchain.c` discussion recommended `void it_802BBAEC(UNK_T arg0, int arg1, float arg2)` until `arg0` could be identified.
Small decomp passes can produce useful struct knowledge: even single-field or padded item-var structs can replace raw unknown offsets and unlock clean `GET_ITEM(gobj)` expressions.
A match is not the same as a correct type model. Review comments repeatedly addressed signatures, argument propagation, and casts rather than instruction-level matching.
naming_conventions: Kept existing item callback naming patterns such as `it_3F14_LogicN_PickedUp`, `Dropped`, `Thrown`, `EnteredAir`, `DmgReceived`, `Reflected`, `ShieldBounced`, and `EvtUnk`.
Kept motion callback naming patterns like `itBombhei_UnkMotion6_Anim`, `itBox_UnkMotion6_Anim`, `itFire_UnkMotion2_Phys`, and `itSonans_UnkMotion0_Phys` when behavior was still unnamed.
Used address-based names such as `it_802B26C0`, `it_802C17DC`, `it_802CD4DC`, and `fn_802C28B8` where semantic names were not established.

Added item-specific struct names following existing conventions: `itFireAttributes`, `itLipstickAttributes`, `itSScopeAttributes`, `itDosei_ItemVars`, `itSonans_ItemVars`, and `itPikachutJoltAir_ItemVars`.
Preserved offset-style field names in newly inferred structs (`x0`, `x4`, `xC`, `xDD8`, `xDE4`, `x60`) when semantics were not yet known.
Used `Item_GObj* gobj` and `Item_GObj* ref_gobj` for item callback signatures rather than generic `HSD_GObj*` casts when the surrounding item system made the type clear.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use `tools/easy_funcs.py` with a function-size threshold to find a bounded set of low-risk candidates; this PR used `-S 40` on the item address range and aimed for zero remaining names.
The author described using Codex and Claude for many of the repetitive decomps, guided by written instructions, then reviewing the output manually.
The author mentioned a workflow using `tools/auto_add_includes.py` and `objdiff-cli diff --format diff` to iterate on includes and compare generated code.
For wrapper matches, propagate the same argument list as the callee when the callee expects `Item_GObj*`; do not omit arguments just because `UNK_PARAMS` lets the code compile.
Use callee decompilation or `decomp.py` to verify hidden signatures behind non-decompiled callees before finalizing tiny wrappers.
Recognize recurring short-function patterns: empty phys functions, `return false`, `return true`, call-and-return wrappers, state transition wrappers, and simple item-var assignment or sign flip.

review_feedback.feedback: A surrounding wrapper needed to take `Item_GObj*` because the function it calls takes an item GObj. Reviewer later explained this was found by checking the callee with `decomp.py` and by knowing most item callbacks take a GObj.
Remove unnecessary `HSD_GObj` casts around `it_8026B894`; suggested passing `gobj, ref_gobj` directly.
Pass `gobj` into `it_802CD4FC` from Sonans phys wrappers instead of calling it with no arguments.
Pass `gobj` into `it_802F1344` in the damage-dealt path.
`it_802BBAEC` needed multiple arguments; reviewer recommended `UNK_T arg0, int arg1, float arg2` while arg0 type remained uncertain.
Suggested `ItemLink` as a possible type for the unknown first argument because it has `CollData` at 0x30 and a bitfield at 0x2C, matching the decomp of `it_802BB938`.
Flagged a questionable cast to `Ground_GObj*`; after checking, concluded `grZakoGenerator_801CACB8` should take an `Item_GObj`, so its signature should be updated and the cast dropped.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that update `grZakoGenerator_801CACB8` signature to take `Item_GObj*` and remove Ground_GObj casts.
Search for remaining `UNK_PARAMS` item callbacks that call known functions with omitted arguments.
Audit item `EvtUnk` wrappers around `it_8026B894` for unnecessary `HSD_GObj*` casts.
Search for `it_802BB938` and `it_802BBAEC` to see whether `arg0` was later named `ItemLink` or given a concrete type.
Find uses of `itSonans_ItemVars.x60` and `itDosei_ItemVars.xDE4` to refine field semantics.
Look for future automation/tooling PRs that add or formalize `tools/auto_add_includes.py`.
